script:
Hello, this is The Portfolio Podcast for MEME. Friday — here is your MEME recap. The portfolio closed down nine point seven two percent on the session. Every primary holding finished in the red. It was a broad, punishing decline with nowhere to hide.

Bloom Energy and Nebius Group led the damage. Bloom Energy dropped nearly fifteen percent while Nebius fell more than fifteen percent — together, they carved out the deepest wounds of the session and set the tone for everything that followed.

The Nasdaq closed lower Friday as technology and growth names bore the heaviest selling pressure. Memory chips, AI infrastructure plays, and clean energy stocks all faced coordinated outflows. No major macro data point was released Friday, though the broader tape reflected a risk-off rotation as investors trimmed high-beta positions heading into the weekend.

Bloom Energy is the top story on Friday. The company fell fourteen point nine one percent in a sector-wide fuel cell and hydrogen selloff that took down the entire space. FuelCell Energy dropped nine percent. Plug Power slipped four percent. According to Yahoo Finance reporting, the move illustrates the fragility of parabolic rallies when market sentiment shifts — even when the underlying bull case remains structurally intact. A Seeking Alpha piece published Friday morning, before the selloff deepened, argued that Bloom Energy is a critical AI infrastructure enabler positioned to address hyperscaler power constraints — rating the stock a buy. A separate Yahoo Finance note highlighted clean energy and battery storage gaining momentum from AI power demand and grid expansion. The tension here is real: the long-term narrative around data center power demand has not changed, but Friday's price action shows that narrative alone cannot hold a position against a macro sentiment shift. Bloom Energy carries five point nine six percent of this portfolio. The earnings report for the quarter ended June twenty twenty-six is the next hard catalyst to watch.

Applied Optoelectronics was down ten point six zero percent on the session. No company-specific headline drove the move — this appears to be sector-driven selling across optical and networking names. At seven point four three percent of the portfolio, it is the largest single position and its decline was the heaviest contributor to today's damage in absolute weight terms. Watch for any order flow commentary tied to AI networking buildouts in the week ahead.

Turning to Redwire Corporation, down six point three six percent. The space and defense industrials sector faced pressure Friday, partly linked to SpaceX-related sentiment. Yahoo Finance reported that SpaceX shares — which joined the public markets last month — got cut roughly in half after a broader industry trade rolled over. Redwire holds six point nine seven percent of the portfolio. A Seeking Alpha piece published early Friday morning called Redwire a speculative buy after the pullback, citing innovative space technologies and high upside potential for aggressive growth investors.

AST SpaceMobile was down five point zero four percent. Yahoo Finance tied the move to broad space sector jitters ahead of a Starship test flight, noting SpaceX fell five percent and Rocket Lab fell seven percent on the same sentiment. AST SpaceMobile represents six point two nine percent of the portfolio. The Starship launch outcome is a direct near-term watch item.

Over at Nebius Group, down fifteen point zero two percent — the steepest single-session decline in the portfolio on Friday. Yahoo Finance noted that Nebius fell while Snowflake climbed and Cloudflare barely moved, flagging divergence in how investors are treating AI cloud exposure during volatility spikes. A separate piece compared Nebius against CoreWeave, noting that Nvidia backs both but CoreWeave's faster growth and cleaner balance sheet are drawing preference. Nebius sits at six point zero one percent of the portfolio.

Meanwhile, TeraWulf fell seven point nine three percent. The bitcoin mining and AI compute infrastructure name moved in line with broader technology selling. No company-specific headline was available. TeraWulf carries five point five zero percent of the portfolio. Hash rate data and any infrastructure partnership announcements remain the key near-term signals.

Lumentum Holdings was down eight point four seven percent Friday. The optical components and photonics name sold off alongside the broader networking and photonics space. No company-specific news was available. At five point five zero percent of the portfolio, the name sits alongside TeraWulf at equal weight. AI data center capex signals from major hyperscalers remain the key driver to monitor.

Iren Limited dropped eight point six five percent. The neocloud and bitcoin mining operator moved lower in a session that punished both AI infrastructure and digital asset adjacent names. A Yahoo Finance piece comparing Iren against Nebius was published Friday, framing the two as competing neocloud bets with different infrastructure philosophies. Iren carries five point zero zero percent of the portfolio.

Sandisk Corporation closed down ten point seven nine percent. Yahoo Finance flagged Sandisk as one of the standout decliners in Friday's session, with memory chip names broadly under pressure as risk appetite weakened. AI memory stocks led the chip selloff according to multiple sources. Sandisk sits at five point zero zero percent of the portfolio.

Three items to watch in the next forty-eight hours: First, the Starship test flight launch window opening Friday evening — outcome directly affects AST SpaceMobile and Redwire sentiment. Second, any weekend commentary from hyperscalers on AI infrastructure spending, which feeds directly into Applied Optoelectronics and Lumentum. Third, Bloom Energy's upcoming quarterly earnings release, which will test whether the AI power demand thesis translates into hard revenue numbers.

This is The Portfolio Podcast for MEME. We will be back same time the next business day with tomorrow's news. Watch the Starship launch result closely — space sector sentiment heading into Monday's open could hinge on it.
